pages.raw_text:
The Press leaves that to the pub
lie to say who is wrong.


Here Is another nice point to be
taken into consideration also,
meanwhile not forgetting the city
halls gallant fight to save the COM
pany the expense Of replacing the
guaranteed" Howard street span.


Tuesday evening Commissioner
ONO announced Enthusiastically that
the Hangman creek bridge had be-
come unsafe. went into the matter
so far as to state that but for
the fortunate discovery of Foreman
Mccormick 1t would have fallen be-
fore the end of the week. He ex-
planned that this bridge had never
been considered safe for the traf-
fic it now bears.


Now, is the result of a meeting
between Mayor Daggett, the COM
missioners and the city engineer
at the city hall yesterday, the
bridge is found to be perfectly safe
with the amount of work possible
to do on it in a few hours.


Engineer Mcintyre finds that
much Of the lumber ordered to keep
the bridge from going down before
the end of the week" was unneces-
sary and cancelled the order. And
Commissioner ONO also has had a
change of heart and talks of mis-
understanding being responsible for
his ordering the material on hand"
He says lit was agreed today that
the high trestle, which is new, is
perfectly safe" with the lining up
and bracing that was done.


Apparently the city administra.
ton can move in hurry when
there is a prospect of a Washington
Water Power car line being fenced
off bridge, even though a city
official does make a slip and pub
licly pronounces the same unsafe,
notwithstanding the guarantees"
to the contrary.
